metadata.title: Research Cancer Staging Logic with Claude: Why TNM Plus Molecular Modifiers Changed the Story
metadata.color_semantics: teardown: INK #2A1A0E body text; CRIMSON #C8102E key finding/vulnerability; SLATE #545454 structure/neutral
metadata.note: CLI video REQUIRED spine: B00 intro · B01 PROBLEM · B02 ASK · B03 CODE · B04 OUTPUT(manim) · B05 CHANGE · B06 OUTPUT(slate) · B07 SUMMARY · B08 NEXT STEPS · B09 outro. Source: cancer-research/chapters/03-molecular-diagnostics-staging-and-the-liquid-biopsy.md

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Same tumor anatomy. Different stage. Different treatment.

Two patients have the same T2N0M0 breast cancer. One is classified as Stage IIA. The other, thanks to a genomic test score, is Stage IA. Same anatomy. Different prognosis. Different treatment. What changed? The AJCC eighth edition incorporated molecular biology into staging — and the decision that follows is whether to add chemotherapy or omit it.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE — problem card: same T2N0M0; two different stages; the molecular modifier that splits the decision

beats.narration_text: In the terminal: ask Claude to work through the specific case — T2N0M0, ER-positive, HER2-negative, Oncotype DX score ten — and show how anatomic stage diverges from prognostic stage.

props.command:
claude "Explain how AJCC 8th edition breast cancer staging
incorporates hormone receptor status, HER2, and
Oncotype DX recurrence score into prognostic stage
groups. Give a specific worked example: T2N0M0,
ER+, HER2-, Oncotype DX score 10. What is the
anatomic stage? What is the prognostic stage?
What treatment decision does this staging difference
change, and what is the TAILORx evidence?"

beats.role_note: terminal (Onda) — Python script reading Claude response
beats.narration_text: Read it. Anatomic stage from T2N0M0: Stage IIA. Add molecular modifiers — ER-positive, HER2-negative, Oncotype DX recurrence score ten — prognostic stage downgrade to IA. TAILORx trial: in ER-positive HER2-negative N0 patients with Oncotype DX twenty-five or below, no benefit from adjuvant chemotherapy. The genomic score changed the treatment.

props.code:
# staging_case.py -- Claude Code output
CASE = {
  "T": "T2", "N": "N0", "M": "M0",
  "ER": "positive", "HER2": "negative",
  "oncotype_dx": 10,
}
# Anatomic stage: T2N0M0 → Stage IIA
ANATOMIC = "Stage IIA"
# Prognostic stage modifiers (AJCC 8th ed Table):
# ER+, HER2-, Oncotype DX ≤25 (postmenopausal)
# → downgrade to prognostic Stage IA
PROGNOSTIC = "Stage IA (downgrade)"
# TAILORx evidence:
# Oncotype DX ≤25: no benefit from adjuvant chemo
# (Sparano et al. NEJM 2018, n=9,719)
TREATMENT_CHANGE = "Chemotherapy OMITTED"
print(f"Anatomic stage:   {ANATOMIC}")
print(f"Prognostic stage: {PROGNOSTIC}")
print(f"Treatment:        {TREATMENT_CHANGE}")
print(f"Evidence:         TAILORx (NEJM 2018)")

beats.narration_text: The convergence tree. T2, N0, M0 merge into anatomic Stage IIA. Then molecular modifiers branch off: ER-positive, HER2-negative, Oncotype DX score ten. These converge with the anatomic stage into prognostic Stage IA — a downgrade. The treatment decision that changes is chemotherapy omission, validated by TAILORx.
beats.visual_intent: Manim: B04_StagingTree — T/N/M nodes converge to anatomic stage, molecular modifiers branch to prognostic stage

beats.narration_text: Apply the same framework to prostate cancer — T2c N0 M0 with PSA seven and Gleason three-plus-four — and show how PSA and grade modify anatomic stage.

props.command:
claude "Apply the same framework to prostate cancer:
T2cN0M0 with PSA 7 and Gleason 3+4 (Grade Group 2).
What is the anatomic stage? How do PSA and grade
group modify it to produce the AJCC 8th edition
prognostic stage group? What treatment decision
is associated with this prognostic group?"

beats.narration_text: Prostate T2cN0M0: anatomic Stage II. With PSA seven and Gleason three-plus-four, AJCC eighth edition places this in prognostic Stage IIB. That prognostic group informs the decision between active surveillance and radical treatment. The same principle: anatomy tells you where the tumor is, biology tells you what it will do.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE — prostate staging example: T2cN0M0 → Stage II anatomic → IIB prognostic with PSA+Gleason

beats.narration_text: Staging is a vocabulary for comparing populations. The AJCC decision to incorporate molecular biology into staging is an acknowledgment that anatomy understates how much biology determines what happens to the patient. Same tumor, same anatomy, different molecular profile — the prognosis can be categorically different, and so can the treatment.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE — summary card: anatomy = where the tumor is; biology = what it does; AJCC 8th integrates both

beats.narration_text: When interpreting a cancer stage, distinguish anatomic stage from prognostic stage. For breast cancer, check whether Oncotype DX or a comparable genomic classifier has been applied. For prostate, check whether PSA and grade group are incorporated. The molecular modifiers are not optional footnotes — in AJCC eighth edition, they are part of the stage.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE — next steps: anatomic vs prognostic stage checklist; Oncotype DX for breast; PSA/grade for prostate

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
